title: Mohrgan Fel
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Mohrgan.jpg]] |name=Mohrgan Fel |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[130 ABY]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|II|323|Mohrgan}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Tan |cyber= |families=[[Fel dynasty]] |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]][[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] **[[Imperial Knight]]s **[[Roan Fel]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Imperial Knights! Save your Emperor!|Mohrgan Fel|}} '''Mohrgan Fel''' was an [[Imperial Knight]] and the cousin of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]. He served his cousin loyally as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and when the [[One Sith|Sith]] emerged during the [[Sith–Imperial War]], he longed to match his skills against theirs. In [[130 ABY]] he got his chance, [[Death/Legends|giving his own life]] in [[Attack on Emperor Fel|combat]] against [[Darth Krayt]] to defend a [[Roan Fel's double|double of Roan Fel]], which gave Roan enough time to escape. ==Biography== {{Quote|Mohrgan… the others—!''"
"''I felt it too. We will honor their sacrifices and escape.|Antares Draco and Roan Fel|}} Mohrgan Fel''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' was born into the [[Fel dynasty|Fel family]], the ruling dynasty of the [[Fel Empire|Empire]]. His cousin [[Roan Fel]] was heir to the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|throne]]. [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] like his cousin, Mohrgan was trained as an [[Imperial Knight]], eventually becoming one of Roan's chief [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] and a close confidante when Roan ascended to the throne. In [[127 ABY]], Roan Fel, under pressure from the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], led the Empire to [[Sith–Imperial War|war]] against the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] over the failure of the [[Ossus Project]]. The [[One Sith|Sith]] emerged on the Empire's side, something which displeased the emperor but, due to [[Politics/Legends|political]] realities, he could not prevent. Mohrgan Fel, much like his cousin,[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] personally detested the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. When the Galactic Alliance [[Battle of Caamas|surrendered]] in [[130 ABY]] and the Sith led a [[Massacre at Ossus|massacre]] of the [[New Jedi Order]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] against Roan's orders, Roan took counsel with Mohrgan and [[Antares Draco]], another Imperial Knight and Roan's former apprentice. [[File:MohrganFelDeath-Legacy2006-8.png|thumb|left|180px|Fel's death at the hands of Darth Krayt]] Roan Fel called a meeting of the Moffs and ordered Mohrgan Fel and Draco to watch [[High Moff]] [[Morlish Veed]] and [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] closely, as he suspected them of treachery. At that moment, the Imperial Knights [[Force sense/Legends|felt]] a presence in the room and turned to find Calixte lurking behind a curtain. Mohrgan [[Telekinesis/Legends|restrained]] her with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as Roan interrogated her as to her purpose. She revealed that [[Darth Krayt]], leader of the Sith, intended to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Emperor Roan Fel at the meeting and place himself on the throne. It was in her interests for Roan to survive, as it would keep her and Veed useful to the Sith. Roan believed her and Mohrgan let her go. The Sith would expect Roan at the meeting, which was swiftly upcoming; Mohrgan Fel volunteered to lead the Imperial Knight bodyguard surrounding a [[Roan Fel's double|double of Roan]] kept on for such circumstances. Fel was confident that he could outfight the Sith; he had wanted to prove himself in combat against them since the beginning of the war. Meanwhile, Roan and Draco escaped through a tunnel route. At the meeting, Krayt and a Sith escort entered as Veed was speaking. As expected, Krayt moved to [[Attack on Emperor Fel|strike down]] the Emperor's double. Fel led the three other Imperial Knights in a fierce defense, but the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] was able to quickly [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|defeat]] all four on his own. Fel was the last to die, [[Cho mai/Legends|disarmed]] and [[Shiak|stabbed]] through the chest by Krayt. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|They'll expect me—and a contingent of Imperial Knights—to appear at the council…''"
"I'll'' lead them—and your ''double''. I've ''wanted'' to test myself against the Sith! They'll have lost the element of surprise. We'll fight our way free and join you!''"
"''Good. That would be good. Just… do not ''underestimate'' them, cousin.|Roan Fel and Mohrgan Fel|}} [[File:ImperialKnightsSlain-SWLEG01.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fel leads the Imperial Knights against Darth Krayt.]] Mohrgan Fel was extremely loyal to his cousin Roan, willing to risk death in order to save his Emperor. He was also extremely confident in his own abilities, leading him to underestimate Darth Krayt before the battle that led to his death. He did not care for the Sith any more than his cousin did and was eager for a chance to prove himself against them, though he ultimately did not fare well against Krayt. ==Powers and abilities== Fel was a trained Imperial Knight, and could use the Force. Fel was able to sense the presence of others as well as use telekinesis, as when he picked Calixte up and slammed her into a wall, pinning her with the Force. He felt that his own combat skills were formidable, though he was ultimately unable to hold his own against Darth Krayt. ==Behind the scenes== Mohrgan Fel initially appeared as an unnamed Imperial Knight defending the Roan Fel decoy in [[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]], which was written by [[John Ostrander]], illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]]. and released on [[June 21]], [[2006]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54719/star_wars_legacy_2006_1|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #1}} In [[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]], which was written by Ostrander, illustrated by [[Adam DeKraker]], and released on [[February 14]], [[2007]],{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54380/star_wars_legacy_2006_8|text=Star Wars: Legacy (2006) #8}} he was identified as Mohrgan. Fel's last name was confirmed by the [[2009]] reference work ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''. ==Appearances== *[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] {{1stID|Mohrgan}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mohrgan Fel}} *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|89|The Art of War (Star Wars Insider 89)}} {{Po}} *''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{1stID|Mohrgan Fel}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Mohrgan Fel |de=Mohrgan Fel |es=Mohrgan Fel |pl=Mohrgan Fel }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fel, Mohrgan}} [[Category:Bodyguards]] [[Category:Fel family]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Knights]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Nobility]] [[Category:Personnel of the Fel Empire]]
